description_long: This object is a large, double-hulled canoe structure with four pointed bows, constructed primarily from wood with visible slatted decks and latticework railings. Each hull features a flat deck area with a partial overhead pergola-style covering, providing shade or structural support. The central section connects the two hulls with a wooden bridge, and the open deck spaces are covered by taut, light-colored fabric canopies. The design suggests a focus on stability and group transport, with ample space for multiple passengers or cargo. The wood is a warm brown, and the canopies are a pale gray or white, creating a visually striking contrast. The overall build is robust, with clear walkable surfaces and multiple entry points.
description: A large, double-hulled wooden canoe with slatted decks, lattice railings, and pale fabric canopies, designed for group transport and stability.
description_view_0: From this view, the pointed bows and the symmetry of the double hulls are prominent, with the canopies and pergola structures clearly visible.
description_view_1: This side view highlights the wide, flat decks, the connecting bridge, and the detailed latticework along the sides.
description_view_2: The rear view shows the open deck spaces and the parallel arrangement of the hulls, with the canopies stretching across the top.
description_view_3: Another side view, similar to view 1, emphasizing the walkable decks, central bridge, and the overall width of the structure.
